extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ won the 36km ITT in Pau by 25 seconds over BEVIN, taking the red jersey and putting 3:06 into QUINTANA.
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR at 20 years old finished top-15 in ITT, revealing GC threat potential earlier than expected in Grand Tours.
QUINTANA lost 3:06 to ROGLIČ despite targeting two-minute maximum loss, showing pre-race ITT damage estimates are often optimistic.
BEVIN finishing second moved him to second overall, demonstrating ITT specialists can GC position via single-day performances.
LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel motivated by ROGLIČ chasing visually from behind, suggesting psychological boost from visible pursuit in ITTs.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Won ITT by 25 seconds at 46+ km/h average, took red jersey with dominant performance establishing GC control.
rider_signals.BEVIN Patrick: positive: Second on stage moved him to second overall, showing CCC rider emerging as unexpected GC contender via ITT strength.
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: Lost red jersey with 3:06 deficit to ROGLIČ, exceeding his stated two-minute maximum acceptable loss target.
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: Top-15 ITT finish in first Grand Tour at age 20 reveals earlier-than-anticipated GC threat development timeline.
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: neutral: Held fourth overall by five seconds, maintained position but lost over two minutes despite visible motivation from ROGLIČ chasing.
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: positive: Posted 47:32 at 45.7 km/h, establishing himself as emerging generational time trialist at age 24.
rider_signals.BARTA Will: positive: First Grand Tour rider posted 49:17 at 44 km/h, showing immediate professional-level ITT competitiveness.
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: Finished 12th at 1:13 down, holding position but showing age-39 ITT limitations against specialists.
